name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
summary: Marjorie Taylor Greene has consistently demonstrated a pattern of promoting conspiracy theories, bigotry, and violence. She has expressed support for white supremacist and antisemitic views, as well as Islamophobic and transphobic ideologies. Greene has also advocated for the execution of prominent Democratic politicians and has made numerous false claims about various topics, including mass shootings, COVID-19, and the 2020 presidential election. Her behavior has been marked by aggression and a disregard for facts, earning her removal from committee assignments and expulsion from the House Freedom Caucus. Greene's actions and rhetoric have been widely criticized, and her continued presence in public life has been marked by controversy and division.

entries.fact: Marjorie Taylor Greene began writing for the now defunct American Truth Seekers, a conspiracy news website, which published 59 articles by Greene
Marjorie Taylor Greene was a top official of the Family America Project, a conservative group, where members posted death threats against Democrats and bigotry directed at the family of Barack Obama

Marjorie Taylor Greene visited the U.S. Capitol and congressional offices, where she posted a livestream video outside Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's office, calling through the mail slot for Ocasio-Cortez to unlock the door and 'face the American citizens that you serve'
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that representatives Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib were not 'official' representatives because they were sworn in to Congress on the Quran
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Islamophobic, antisemitic, and white supremacist views, including the white genocide conspiracy theory, as well as QAnon and Pizzagate
Greene amplified conspiracy theories that allege government involvement in mass shootings in the United States, implicate the Clinton family in murder, and suggest the attacks of 9/11 were a hoax
Before running for Congress, Greene supported calls to execute prominent Democratic Party politicians, including Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama

Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a gun rights rally in Villanow hosted by Silent No Longer, a group campaigning to make Walker County a Second Amendment sanctuary, and posed for photos with Chester Doles, a 5th-generation Ku Klux Klansman

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted a video on Facebook where she held an AR-15 style rifle and warned antifa groups to 'stay the hell out of Northwest Georgia', which was later taken down by Facebook for violating its terms of service

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote on Twitter that children should not wear masks, calling recommendations by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention 'unhealthy for their psychological, emotional, and educational growth' and 'emasculating' for boys.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a meme on Facebook depicting herself holding an AR-15 style rifle next to a collage of pictures of Ocasio-Cortez, Omar, and Tlaib, with the caption 'Squad's worst nightmare'
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'anti-American', a 'hypocrite', and a 'bitch' during her victory speech
Marjorie Taylor Greene made deceptive claims that her husband Perry's Floyd County voting record revealed voter fraud
As a congresswoman, Greene equated the Democratic Party with Nazis and compared COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the Plan B contraceptive 'kills a baby in the womb'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene raised an objection to counting Michigan's electoral votes during the counting of electoral votes
The U.S. House of Representatives voted to remove Greene from all committee roles in response to her endorsements of political violence
Marjorie Taylor Greene faced a legal challenge to her eligibility based on her alleged involvement in organizing and promoting the 2021 United States Capitol attack
Greene was expelled from the conservative House Freedom Caucus after insulting fellow caucus member Congresswoman Lauren Boebert
Greene unsuccessfully attempted to oust Mike Johnson from his role as Speaker of the House of Representatives
Marjorie Taylor Greene confronted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg near the U.S. Capitol, questioning his gun control activism and calling him a 'coward' for ignoring her
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed David Hogg's gun control activism efforts were funded by billionaire George Soros

Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'The government will never tell me how many guns I can own, and how many bullets I am allowed to fire if someone were to attack me or my kids' at a gun rights rally in Ringgold, Georgia
Marjorie Taylor Greene told viewers that 'the only way you get your freedoms back is it's earned with the price of blood' in an interview with gun activist Chris Dorr
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a face mask while sheltering in place during the riot at the US Capitol
Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that Democrats must be held accountable for the political violence inspired by their rhetoric during the debate to impeach Trump
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely suggested that the rioters at the US Capitol were members of antifa dressed as Trump supporters

Marjorie Taylor Greene retweeted a clip from Fox News host Tucker Carlson claiming that agents of the Federal Bureau of Investigation might have organized and participated in the attack on the Capitol
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment against President Joe Biden, citing alleged abuse of power as the reason for impeachment
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the National Marrow Donor Program bill would fund aborted fetal tissue with taxpayer money
Marjorie Taylor Greene aggressively confronted Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez outside the House chamber, loudly asking why she supported antifa and Black Lives Matter
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the January 6 commission, a proposed commission which would have investigated the attack on the Capitol

Marjorie Taylor Greene called for justice for Ashli Babbitt, a Trump supporter who was fatally shot by a Capitol police officer when she stormed the Capitol and attempted to break into the House chamber
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 21 House Republicans to vote against a resolution to give the Congressional Gold Medal to police officers who defended the U.S. Capitol on January 6
Marjorie Taylor Greene promised that companies that complied with the House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack's request to retain phone records would be 'shut down'
Marjorie Taylor Greene was removed from all committee assignments due to incendiary remarks she had made before her election
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a conference hosted by white supremacist Nick Fuentes, where Fuentes called the January 6 attack 'awesome' and praised Putin and Hitler
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced criticisms of her attendance at the white supremacist conference as 'identity politics' and an attempt to 'cancel' her, and labelled critics 'Pharisees'
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the Freedom Caucus by a vote of its members after calling fellow caucus member Lauren Boebert a 'little bitch' during an argument on the House floor
Marjorie Taylor Greene and Congresswoman Elise Stefanik introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge both of Trump's impeachments
Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Trump's support for Israeli strikes against Iran and opposed the possible involvement of the United States in the war

Marjorie Taylor Greene began supporting the House of Representatives' discharge petition to release the files from the Jeffrey Epstein client list and criticized Trump for actively opposing the database's release
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the Squad supporters of 'terrorism' and 'Hamas' who 'don't belong in Congress' and referred to them as the 'Jihad Squad'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene contended that the Democratic Party is holding Black Americans as 'slaves'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Muslims who believe in Sharia law should not be in the U.S. government.

Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that vaccine passports might be 'Biden's mark of the beast', a reference to the Book of Revelation, echoing a far-right conspiracy theory that getting vaccinated is equivalent to pledging allegiance to the devil.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill in the House, the We Will Not Comply Bill, which sought to ban vaccine passports.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill in the House, the Fire Fauci Bill, which would eliminate Fauci's salary until his successor was confirmed by the Senate.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the Myanmar coup d'état
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for not wearing a mask on the House floor.

Marjorie Taylor Greene called Pelosi 'mentally ill' and compared mask requirements to the Holocaust, saying they are 'exactly the [same] type of abuse' as Jews being 'put in trains and taken to gas chambers in Nazi Germany'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene sent Biden a letter calling for an investigation into Fauci over his statements on the origin of SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19, and referred to COVID-19 as a 'manufactured plague' caused by a 'Chinese-made virus'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene dismissed the COVID-19 variants, including the Delta variant, saying 'no one cares'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted misinformation about the virus, saying it 'is not dangerous for non-obese people and those under 65'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated she would kick out every Chinese person in the United States who is loyal to the Chinese Communist Party and impose strict tariffs on China if she had the authority
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the bipartisan ALLIES Act, which would increase the number of special immigrant visas for Afghan allies of the U.S. military

Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Biden would be 'sending one of his police state friends to your front door' to inquire whether they had received a vaccine, and said 'in the South, we all love our Second Amendment rights, and we're not really big on strangers showing up on our front door'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely said that hospitals were not overwhelmed with afflicted patients, and added 'we're human, we can't live forever, we're going to catch all kinds of diseases and illnesses and other viruses'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene had been fined 20 times for not wearing a mask on the House floor, totaling $48,000 in fines.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the anti-vaccine film Died Suddenly, which promotes misinformation about COVID-19 vaccines and Great Reset conspiracy theories.
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed an amendment to a House foreign aid bill on Ukraine that would require all members of the House voting in favor of the bill to enlist in the Ukrainian military
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the lifting of U.S. sanctions on Russia
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 18 Republicans to vote against admitting Sweden and Finland to NATO
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeatedly refused to address Fauci as Doctor, questioned his medical licence and accused him of covering up COVID-19's origins during a congressional hearing.
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed a defense appropriations amendment that would end U.S. funding of Israel's Iron Dome
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed doubt that the perpetrator of the 2017 Las Vegas shooting acted alone, suggesting it was an attack on the right to bear arms
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the August 2017 Charlottesville white nationalist rally was an 'inside job'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'There's a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to take this global cabal of Satan-worshipping pedophiles out, and I think we have the president to do it'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'many of the things' said on 4chan by the eponymous Q have 'really proven to be true'
Marjorie Taylor Greene speculated that the Pizzagate conspiracy theory is real
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the election of Omar and Tlaib in the 2018 midterm elections was part of 'an Islamic invasion of our government'
Marjorie Taylor Greene co-sponsored the Old Glory Only Bill, a bill to ban U.S. embassies from flying pride flags
Marjorie Taylor Greene displayed a transphobic poster outside her office reading: 'There are TWO genders: Male & Female. Trust The Science!'

Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed replacing the Equality Act with a bill that would exempt nonprofit organizations, allow people to sue the federal government 'if their religious rights are violated', and prevent trans women and girls from participating in women's sports
Marjorie Taylor Greene tried to block the Equality Act while it was being debated on the House floor, saying it would 'destroy God's creation' and 'violate everything we hold dear in God's creation'
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter activists to white nationalist participants at the August 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville, Virginia.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the most mistreated group of people in the United States today are white males.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act, a civil rights and police reform bill, as an 'Anti-Police bill'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene supported a bill by Representative Mary Miller that would bar schools from allowing transgender students to use facilities such as bathrooms and locker rooms that correspond to their gender identity
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial was a result of jury intimidation by Black Lives Matter, which she compared to the Ku Klux Klan.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called transgender women 'men playing dress up' and 'literally having an identity crisis', saying, 'They never will be [women], and I refuse to recognize them that way'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that schools teaching about LGBT people is 'mental/emotional child abuse'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said any Republican who supported the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act was 'a traitor to our party, a traitor to their voters and a traitor to our donors'
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the $1.2 trillion Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act Biden's 'communist takeover of America'
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that straight people would go extinct within less than two centuries due to LGBT-inclusive educators, calling them 'trans terrorists'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene argued that Canada's proposed gun control laws would leave Canada 'weak and vulnerable to being invaded and easily taken over by another stronger country'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that 'an entire' pride month celebrating LGBT people 'needs to end'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said the July 4 Highland Park parade shooting was 'designed to make Republicans go along with more gun control'
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced the 'Protect Children's Innocence Act', which would make giving gender-affirming care to transgender youth a felony punishable by up to 25 years in prison.
Marjorie Taylor Greene responded to California state senator Scott Wiener by saying that the Protect Children's Innocence Act would stop 'communist groomers' such as Wiener.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a false flag operation
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2018 Stoneman Douglas High School shooting in Parkland, Florida, was an organized false flag operation
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for a conspiracy theory that a plane did not hit the Pentagon during the September 11 attacks

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote that 'I am told that Nancy Pelosi tells Hillary Clinton several times a month that 'we need another school shooting' in order to persuade the public to want strict gun control'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'the so-called plane that crashed into the Pentagon' and that 'it's odd there's never any evidence shown for a plane in the Pentagon'

Greene's Facebook account responded to a post asking about hanging Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama with 'Stage is being set. Players are being put in place. We must be patient. This must be done perfectly or liberal judges would let them off.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg had been replaced in public appearances by a body double
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg '#littleHitler'
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced David Hogg as an 'idiot' who is trained 'like a dog'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said Nancy Pelosi is guilty of treason and that treason is a crime punishable by death in a Facebook video
Greene suggested that Nancy Pelosi would either be killed or imprisoned for treason in Facebook live-streamed videos
Marjorie Taylor Greene live-streamed a video targeting Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, calling him a "coward" and falsely claiming he is funded by George Soros
Greene expressed racist, antisemitic, and Islamophobic views in videos published during the Republican primary election
Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a video promoting the white genocide conspiracy theory, which claims that Zionists are conspiring to flood Europe with migrants to replace the native white populations

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a conspiracy theory about the Camp Fire, a deadly Californian wildfire, suggesting that it could have been caused by "what looked like lasers or beams of blue light" from "space solar generators" in a scheme involving California governor Jerry Brown, companies PG&E, Rothschild & Co, and Solaren
Marjorie Taylor Greene agreed with a conspiracy theory known as Frazzledrip, which asserts that there is a video of Hillary Clinton and her assistant Huma Abedin murdering a child in a satanic ritual

Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was locked for 12 hours for multiple violations of Twitter's civic integrity policy, based on her posts including false claims about voting fraud and statements blaming electoral officials in Georgia
Marjorie Taylor Greene's Twitter account was temporarily suspended for violating Twitter's policies, including spreading COVID-19 vaccine misinformation
The full House voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from her committee assignments with a vote of 230 to 199, with 11 Republicans joining all the Democrats.
Marjorie Taylor Greene attended the America First Political Action Conference, which was criticized by Senate minority leader Mitch McConnell for having 'white supremacists or anti-Semitism'
Greene promoted the conspiracy theory that the U.S. government is planning to force Americans to eat fake meat grown in a 'peach tree dish' by Bill Gates

Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'People die in the cold. This Earth warming, and carbon, is actually healthy for us. It helps us to feed people, it keeps people alive. ... We need to hold Democrats accountable and defund all of their climate garbage'
Greene said that Democrats had designed the killing of Ayman al-Zawahiri to boost Biden in the midterms

Marjorie Taylor Greene repeated her suggestion of a 'national divorce' between red states and blue states, and that red states should disenfranchise people who move there from blue states for a period of five years

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted online 'a map of hurricane affected areas with an overlay of electoral map by political party shows how hurricane devastation could affect the election', then wrote 'Yes they can control the weather … It's ridiculous for anyone to lie and say it can't be done'
Marjorie Taylor Greene apologized for using the word 'retard' to refer to individuals with Down syndrome, saying it was a slang word and she was sorry if it was offensive to anyone.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was barred from blocking anyone on her public Twitter or any other social media account while in office and was forced to pay $10,000 to cover legal fees for MeidasTouch
Marjorie Taylor Greene broke Georgia law by claiming homestead exemptions on two properties, when only one may legally be claimed.

Common Cause filed an FEC complaint against Marjorie Taylor Greene for an alleged violation of McCain–Feingold campaign finance provisions, accusing her of 'implicit' unlawful solicitation of unlimited contributions in an advertisement appearance made on behalf of a super PAC.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was permanently suspended for spreading COVID-19 vaccine misinformation
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to the police guarding the Capitol building as 'gazpacho police', confusing the cold Spanish soup with the Gestapo.
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed a federal lawsuit to block a state law that allowed a candidate to be removed from a ballot after review by an administrative law judge and the Georgia Secretary of State
Marjorie Taylor Greene accused Republicans who supported then-Supreme Court nominee Ketanji Brown Jackson of being 'pro-pedophile'.
A federal judge denied Marjorie Taylor Greene's challenge to the state law, requiring her to give evidence four days later

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops, accusing them of 'destroying' America with their work regarding immigrants and refugees and claiming that Satan was 'controlling the church'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the Robb Elementary School shooter had engaged in cross-dressing, echoing similar claims by right-wing politicians and media figures that the shooter was transgender
Twitter labeled two tweets by Marjorie Taylor Greene as violating their rules on hateful conduct for misgendering and deadnaming Assistant Secretary for Health Rachel Levine

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the presence of the Georgia Guidestones, a megalithic granite monument, and said ecumenical texts inscribed on it represented a nefarious future of "population control" as envisioned by the "hard left"
Marjorie Taylor Greene targeted and misgendered congresswoman Sarah McBride in a series of tweets, labeling her a 'groomer'
Marjorie Taylor Greene made a statement about Mark Levin, saying he has a 'micropenis' in support of Megyn Kelly
